place: Keansburg borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Keansburg was formed as a borough on March 26, 1917, from parts of Middletown and Raritan Townships after a referendum.
The area was once called Tanner's Landing, named for a pier on what is now Main Street, until about 1820.
In 1910 the Keansburg Steamboat Company began ferrying New Yorkers to the borough, marking the start of steamboat transport across Raritan Bay.
The Keansburg Waterfront Public Library opened in 2004 after a town‑wide effort to provide a high‑quality community library.
The borough’s beaches give access to Raritan Bay, offering swimming and sunbathing opportunities.
Keansburg’s history includes the Gehlhaus brothers, who founded the Steamboat Company and later owned the amusement park that once drew crowds in the 1980s.
